channel.title: Fahlevi Thing
channel.description: A personal archive of thoughts, investment insights, and stock analysis by Reza Fahlevi.

item.title: The 30-Day Fix: Can Indonesia's Free Meals Program Reform Itself?
item.description: Eighteen months into Indonesia's most ambitious social program, President Prabowo has given BGN thirty days to overhaul MBG's governance. What happens in that window will determine whether the program survives as a credible investment in human capital.

item.title: Money That Outlives Its Source: The Quiet Logic of Sovereign Wealth Funds
item.description: Most of Norway's sovereign wealth was never under the seabed; it was created by markets, by capital planted early and left alone to grow. That fact is the entire argument for sovereign wealth funds, and the right lens for judging Danantara.

item.title: The Tesla Playbook
item.description: Tesla is worth $1.5 trillion on shrinking profits. This is the playbook (older than GoTo, older than Uber, written first by Amazon) that lets a company command a valuation priced years ahead of its earnings.

item.title: Is SpaceX Following Gojek's Playbook?
item.description: SpaceX raised $75 billion at a $1.77 trillion valuation while disclosing a $4.9 billion loss. Indonesia watched a strikingly similar playbook with GoTo in 2022. The two stories rhyme, but they diverge in ways that matter.

item.title: How Gojek Got Big by Selling the Next Round, Not the Next Profit
item.description: Gojek lost money almost every year yet grew from a $1.3B valuation to $31B at IPO. Here is how a startup sells the next round, not the next profit, and what Amazon's story actually teaches.

item.title: Why Do Employees Need a Pension Fund If They Can Build One Themselves?
item.description: When it comes to investment returns, a disciplined employee today can get remarkably close to what a professional pension fund delivers, and sometimes do better. But matching its certainty is the part that's genuinely difficult.

item.title: Echoes Beneath the Thames: How a 3.2-mile experiment in 1890 London built the operating system of the modern city
item.description: On 4 November 1890, the City & South London Railway became the first deep-level electric tube railway in the world. Within a century, more than two hundred cities on six continents would be built around its logic.

item.title: Echoes of the New Order: Why Indonesia's New Export Body Stirs Old Anxieties in the Capital Market
item.description: On 20 May 2026, President Prabowo Subianto used his state-of-the-economy address to the House (DPR) to unveil a new Government Regulation on the Governance of Natural-Resource Exports.

item.title: Executive Summary: Indonesia's Macroeconomic Positioning in the G20 (Q1 2026)
item.description: Indonesia opened 2026 with solid first-quarter results: year-on-year (YoY) GDP growth reached 5.61%, inflation remained well-contained, and Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) inflows held strong.

item.title: Part 3 of 3: The Age of Levers, How FC Barcelona Mortgaged Its Future
item.description: In the summer of 2021, Joan Laporta returned to the FC Barcelona presidency with a mandate that would have seemed almost paradoxical to the club's founders: to save a democratic, member-owned institution by deploying the financial instruments of Wall Street.

item.title: Part 2 of 3: From the Blank Jersey to Qatar Airways, Commercial Pivot
item.description: In the spring of 2009, FC Barcelona played football that many who witnessed it still struggle to describe in purely sporting terms. The club was at the apex of a philosophy: tiki-taka possession, pressed with geometric precision...

item.title: Part 1 of 3: "More Than a Club", How FC Barcelona Was Built as a Democratic Institution
item.description: There is a phrase in Catalan, Més que un club, that FC Barcelona has carried for over half a century. Translated, it means "More than a club." But what does that actually mean? How does a football club transcend the sport itself...

item.title: The WIFI Chronicles: A Journey of Conviction, Caution, and Market Timing
item.description: My journey with WIFI (PT Solusi Sinergi Digital Tbk.) began with a simple quantitative screen on Stockbit. I was searching for undervalued gems, and WIFI caught my eye with its low PBV and PE ratios...

item.title: MBG Part 5: The Grand Vision & Long-Term Impacts
item.description: If Indonesia successfully navigates the current implementation hurdles and achieves a 'zero mistake' operational state, the MBG program transforms into a powerful engine for national restructuring...

item.title: MBG Part 4: Safety, Quality, and the "Zero Mistake" Mandate
item.description: As Indonesia moves toward its ambitious goal of serving over 82.9 million beneficiaries by 2029, the National Nutrition Agency (BGN) has had to confront immediate systemic failures...

item.title: MBG Part 3: Navigating the Implementation Minefield
item.description: While the vision of a universal school meal program is theoretically 'genius,' the transition from policy paper to a child’s plate is fraught with systemic obstacles...

item.title: MBG Part 2: The Nutritional Gap & Per Capita Consumption
item.description: To understand why a national school meal program is hailed as a 'genius' intervention, one must examine the empirical data regarding what actually reaches the plates of citizens...

item.title: Program Makan Bergizi Gratis (MBG): The “Genius” Initiative Facing Implementation Challenges
item.description: School nutrition policies have evolved from simple public health interventions into strategic pillars for human capital development and geopolitical stability. Globally, providing school meals is no longer just an act of philanthropy...

item.title: The Glitch in the Archive
item.description: The archive grows heavier by the hour. New lines of code, fresh paragraphs, Another article was pushed to the live server. The index page scrolls endlessly down, A monument of sudden, manic focus.

item.title: The Portfolio War – A Deep Dive into Hajj Fund Strategies (Malaysia vs. Indonesia)
item.description: While Part 1 explored institutional structures, Part 2 dissects balance sheets. Malaysia’s TH operates as a diversified multi-asset endowment, while Indonesia’s BPKH functions closer to a sovereign debt accumulator.
